ArchitectureNotes.objective: Clean layered architecture: transport adapters depend on shared core, never on each other.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No distributed or multi-node execution patterns.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Backend selection at startup must handle missing runtimes gracefully.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Single inference guard serializes all generation requests across adapters.

contract.purpose: Parse and validate environment-based runtime configuration for all components.

export-CoreSettings.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all shared runtime settings

export-parse_core_settings_from_env.PURPOSE: Parse environment variables into typed settings dict

contract.purpose: Define typed domain error hierarchy for all core operations.

export-CoreError.PURPOSE: Base exception for all domain errors

export-ModelNotAvailableError.PURPOSE: Raised when requested model cannot be found

export-InferenceBusyError.PURPOSE: Raised when inference slot is occupied

export-JobNotFoundError.PURPOSE: Raised when job ID is not in store

export-RateLimitExceededError.PURPOSE: Raised when rate limit policy is violated

export-QuotaExceededError.PURPOSE: Raised when quota policy is violated

contract.purpose: Provide request context propagation, structured logging, and timing utilities.

export-log_event.PURPOSE: Emit structured JSON log with request context

export-bind_request_context.PURPOSE: Set request ID in context var

export-get_request_id.PURPOSE: Read current request ID from context

contract.purpose: Collect and expose counters, gauges, and timing metrics for runtime operations.
Define command DTOs, job contracts, and result types for the TTS domain.
Register, select, and manage TTS inference backends (MLX and Torch).

export-BackendRegistry.PURPOSE: Backend selection and model spec resolution

export-LoadedModelHandle.PURPOSE: Handle to a loaded model with metadata

contract.purpose: Provide local implementations for job execution, audio I/O, admission control, and concurrency.

export-LocalBoundedExecutionManager.PURPOSE: Local async job execution with bounded concurrency

export-convert_audio_to_wav_if_needed.PURPOSE: Audio format normalization for clone flows

export-build_rate_limiter.PURPOSE: Factory for rate limiter from settings

export-build_quota_guard.PURPOSE: Factory for quota guard from settings

contract.purpose: Discover, validate, preload, and serve model handles through the selected backend.

export-ModelRegistry.PURPOSE: High-level model discovery and loading facade

contract.purpose: Coordinate inference for custom, design, and clone synthesis modes.

export-TTSService.PURPOSE: Core synthesis coordinator with inference guard

contract.purpose: Provide application-level facade for TTS operations including job execution gateway and admission control.

export-TTSApplicationService.PURPOSE: High-level synthesis facade for transport adapters

M-BOOTSTRAP.NAME: CoreBootstrap

contract.purpose: Assemble the full CoreRuntime from settings by wiring all components together.

export-CoreRuntime.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass holding all wired runtime components

export-build_runtime.PURPOSE: Factory function that assembles CoreRuntime from settings

M-BOOTSTRAP.depends: M-CONFIG, M-BACKENDS, M-MODEL-REGISTRY, M-TTS-SERVICE, M-APPLICATION, M-INFRASTRUCTURE, M-METRICS

contract.purpose: Expose TTS capabilities through FastAPI HTTP endpoints with auth, rate limiting, and error handling.
Expose TTS capabilities through Telegram bot commands with allowlist, rate limiting, and delivery persistence.

export-main.PURPOSE: Telegram bot entry point and polling loop

contract.purpose: Provide interactive local CLI for synthesis workflows with optional audio playback.

export-main.PURPOSE: CLI entry point with interactive loop

DF-SYNC-TTS.NAME: Synchronous TTS Generation
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-1: Server receives HTTP request, validates input, applies auth and rate limiting
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-2: Server bootstrap resolves CoreRuntime and calls TTSApplicationService
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-3: TTSApplicationService dispatches to TTSService with the appropriate command
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-4: TTSService acquires inference guard, resolves model via ModelRegistry
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-6: Audio result is read, optionally persisted, and returned as GenerationResult
DF-SYNC-TTS.step-7: Server returns audio bytes in HTTP response
DF-SYNC-TTS.evidence: tts.generation.completed log marker, audio bytes in response

DF-ASYNC-JOB.NAME: Async Job Submission and Retrieval
DF-ASYNC-JOB.step-1: Server receives job submission, validates idempotency key
DF-ASYNC-JOB.step-5: On completion, client retrieves audio via GET /api/v1/tts/jobs/{job_id}/result
DF-ASYNC-JOB.evidence: jobs.submitted counter, jobs.completed counter, job status transitions

DF-CLONE-TTS.NAME: Voice Clone with Reference Audio
DF-CLONE-TTS.TRIGGER: Clone request with reference audio file
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-1: Transport adapter receives reference audio and text
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-2: TTSService copies reference audio to temporary directory
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-4: Backend synthesizes clone with normalized reference
DF-CLONE-TTS.step-5: Temporary files are cleaned up in finally block

DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.NAME: Telegram Bot TTS Flow
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-1: Polling loop receives update from Telegram API
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-2: Dispatcher routes to appropriate handler based on command
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-3: Handler validates user allowlist and rate limit
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-4: Handler creates command DTO and submits to job orchestrator
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-5: Job orchestrator polls for completion and delivers audio via sender
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.step-6: Delivery metadata is persisted for recovery after restart
DF-TELEGRAM-TTS.evidence: Delivery store contains completed job entries

DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.NAME: Backend Autoselection at Startup
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-1: BackendRegistry receives list of backend instances and settings
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-2: If backend is explicitly set, select that backend
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-3: If autoselect is enabled, prefer MLX (Apple Silicon) then Torch
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-4: Selected backend loads model manifest and resolves model specs
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.step-5: ModelRegistry applies preload policy if configured
DF-BACKEND-SELECTION.evidence: BackendRegistry selection report, model readiness report

DF-CLI-TTS.NAME: CLI Interactive Synthesis
DF-CLI-TTS.TRIGGER: User enters synthesis command in CLI
DF-CLI-TTS.step-1: CLI runtime reads user input and parses command
DF-CLI-TTS.step-2: CLI creates command DTO from parsed input
DF-CLI-TTS.step-3: TTSApplicationService executes synthesis via core runtime
DF-CLI-TTS.step-4: Audio is optionally saved and/or played via sounddevice

Phase-1.name: Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Core utilities and configuration without external dependencies.
Phase-1.step-5: Command DTOs, job contracts, result types

Phase-2.name: Core Services
Phase-2.goal: Backend selection, model management, and inference coordination.
Phase-2.step-1: Backend registry with MLX and Torch
Phase-2.step-2: Local job execution, audio I/O, admission control

Phase-3.name: Runtime Assembly
Phase-3.goal: Wire all core components into a single runtime.

Phase-4.name: Transport Adapters
Phase-4.goal: Expose TTS through HTTP, Telegram, and CLI.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
